mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to predict positive quarterly returns with a classification accuracy above 55%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% annually during late-cycle expansions indicates capacity constraints that force firms to raise prices, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth predicts positive 20-day returns in late-cycle regimes, or the negative correlation reverses to positive with a p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 3% confirms robust economic expansion that immediately boosts equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters with GDP growth above 3%, or the signal information coefficient remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which raises long-term discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >65% of high-inflation months, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for Fed forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment confirms a healthy labor market expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 20-day returns are negative in >55% of aligned labor market expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth during periods of ultra-low interest rates indicates excessive consumer leverage that inevitably triggers a demand correction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: High retail sales during low-rate periods predict positive 5-day returns with a t-statistic above 2.0, or the negative relationship disappears when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%.
